alerts.title: Israel continues Beirut strikes amid nuclear deal negotiations, killing 3 civilians
alerts.summary: Israeli Defense Force struck Hezbollah positions in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahieh) on June 15, killing at least 3 civilians, marking the first deadly attack since the US-Iran framework MoU announcement (Reuters, NBC News incidents feed). Strikes persist despite Trump's public criticism of Netanyahu and risk jeopardizing the emerging ceasefire framework scheduled for Friday signing.

alerts.title: US-Iran framework deal threatened by Israeli Beirut strikes; Trump criticizes Netanyahu judgment
alerts.summary: President Trump explicitly stated that Israeli actions in Beirut are placing the US-Iran peace agreement at risk and that Netanyahu lacks sound judgment on the matter (Axios, incidents feed). Public rebuke signals deep fracture between US and Israeli leadership over negotiation strategy and de-escalation commitment.

alerts.title: Iran nuclear enrichment at 60% amid deal framework; breakout window remains 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Iran maintains uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 concentration with estimated 1-2 week breakout capability while political framework agreement proceeds toward Friday signing in Switzerland (global_metrics, incidents feed). IAEA board resolution demanding inspection access and uranium stock reporting remains unresolved, indicating nuclear verification remains contentious.

alerts.title: Houthis announce ban on Israeli vessels; expand Red Sea maritime pressure
alerts.summary: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthis announced a ban on Israeli shipping in the Red Sea and conducted recent attacks on Israeli targets, escalating maritime pressure in strategic chokepoint (Reuters, Long War Journal incidents feed). Move signals proxy coordination with broader US-Iran framework de-escalation rhetoric still contested by militant wings.

alerts.title: Rybar reports 60-day ceasefire window with full deal signing set for June 19 in Switzerland
alerts.summary: Rybar Telegram channel (~145K views) reported that US-Iran memorandum was achieved via Pakistan and Qatar mediation, specifying 60-day ceasefire across all fronts including Lebanon, with official signing scheduled for Friday, June 19, 2026 in Switzerland (telegram feed). Timeline aligns with Trump's announced framework but Iranian officials have already signaled skepticism about uranium export provisions.

analysis_note: US-Iran framework deal framework hangs by thread as Israel continues military operations in Lebanon threatening ceasefire; nuclear enrichment remains unresolved and 1-2 week breakout window persists despite diplomatic momentum.
